description_long: This guard rail consists of three horizontal rectangular rails supported by four vertical posts, all constructed from a uniform, light tan material that resembles painted or coated wood or composite. The rails are evenly spaced and attached to the posts with visible joinery, giving the structure a sturdy and functional appearance. The posts extend slightly above and below the rails, suggesting they are designed to be embedded into the ground for stability. The ends of the rails are blunt and squared off, with no visible end treatments or safety features. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for use in parks, sidewalks, or low-traffic urban areas. There are no reflective markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its practical purpose as a barrier.
description: A simple, light tan guard rail with three horizontal rails and four vertical posts, constructed from a wood-like or composite material. The design is utilitarian, with blunt ends and no reflective markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing three evenly spaced horizontal rails attached to four vertical posts, highlighting the full width and structure.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the rails and posts, with minimal depth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the rails and posts from the opposite side, with joinery details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
